58.reason: [Should be 'i = le - 1' instead of 'i = l - 1'; 'l' is length of original string including '.', but 't' has no '.', so 'le' is correct length for 't'. Using 'l-1' may access uninitialized parts of 't' if '.' was present.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be 'i = le - 1' instead of 'i = l - 1'; 'l' is length of original string including '.', but 't' has no '.', so 'le' is correct length for 't'. Using 'l-1' may access uninitialized parts of 't' if '.' was present.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Here, 'i' is set to l-1 (index in s), but then used as index in t, which has length le <= l-1. This leads to out-of-bounds access when l-1 >= le, causing undefined behavior. Should use i = le - 1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be 'i = le - 1' because 't' has length 'le', but 'l' is length of original string including '.', so 'l-1' may exceed 't' bounds if '.' was present. This leads to reading uninitialized memory in 't'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be 'i = le - 1' because 't' has length 'le', not 'l'; using 'l-1' may access uninitialized parts of 't' if the original string had a decimal point, leading to incorrect trailing zero removal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [This should be 'i = le - 1' because 't' is the cleaned string without '.', and its length is 'le', not 'l'. Using 'l-1' may access uninitialized parts of 't' or miss trailing zeros.]
